name: Incline Barbell Bench Press
resistance_profile: Hardest off the upper chest (bottom), easing near lockout; moderate lengthened loading of the chest, with the incline aligning force along the upper (clavicular) fibers.
execution_steps: Set an adjustable bench to about 30 degrees and lie back with your eyes under the bar.
Pinch your shoulder blades down and back, plant your feet, and unrack the bar over your upper chest.
Lower the bar under control to your upper chest near the collarbone.
Press back up and slightly back over the shoulders to lockout without bouncing.
good_when: You want to emphasize the upper (clavicular) chest with a heavy barbell.
You want a progressable pressing lift for upper-chest strength.
bad_when: The bench angle is too steep and the front delts take over (lower the incline).
Your shoulders do not tolerate a fixed bar path (use incline dumbbells).
